$comment: DAY-2 turn spec — the SINGLE source of turn semantics the day-2 runner consumes (author A writes it; author B's runner/scorecard/verify treat it as the only source of truth, hard-coding nothing about recipes). Each turn is a SELF-CONTAINED change applied cumulatively on top of the previous turn's brief, starting from app.turn0_brief. The runner builds turn 0 once, then for each turn: applies brief_patch -> runs skill_only_path (skill-only mode) -> on hybrid mode runs hybrid_path (a Stage-C day2-driver STUB this stage) -> PROBES the live tenant schema for whether the change actually landed (DID-IT-LAND) -> asserts new_capability_assert AND re-runs regression_flows. brief_patch is described declaratively (a precise, machine-parseable instruction) because the day-2 layer being probed differs per turn; the runner edits the per-run brief accordingly. All field types/policies/relation shapes/flow ids/modules below were validated against scripts/lib/brief.mjs via a DRY scaffold-provision (see ../day2/README.md). IMPORTANT — verdicts are EMPIRICAL: the skill_only_path strings below DOCUMENT the steps the runner runs; they are NOT verdict oracles. The runner decides clean/partial/impossible from the live did-it-land probe + the dual-verify, never from any prediction in this file. The `verified_outcome` field on each turn records what a Stage-B baseline ACTUALLY observed on a warm hub (see ../day2/changes.md): additive re-provision is IDEMPOTENT (T1/T2 land clean additive — confirmed in the live tenant: the new column/table/FK all appear); the real wall is a NOT-NULL column added to an ALREADY-POPULATED table (T3 is_published); a no-module flow add (T4) is PARTIAL (Blocks/ops install; the re-provision leg is a no-op since it adds zero modules). The earlier claim that EVERY turn aborts with a duplicate-CREATE-POLICY 'PROVISION-RERUN-001' was FALSIFIED and has been removed.

turns.title: additive column
turns.change_request: Add a prep time (in minutes) field to recipes so a cook can record how long a recipe takes to prepare.
turns.brief_patch: In data_model.tables[name=recipes].fields, append a new field: { name: prep_minutes, type: { name: numeric } } (optional — no `required`, no `default`).
turns.skill_only_path: edit brief -> scaffold-provision -> (cd packages/provision) pnpm run provision. (Steps only — NOT a verdict prediction. The runner decides the verdict from the live did-it-land probe + dual-verify.)
turns.verified_outcome: clean. VERIFIED (Stage-B, warm hub): additive re-provision is IDEMPOTENT — an optional plain column lands skill-only. Confirmed in the live tenant: information_schema.columns shows recipes.prep_minutes present (nullable). provision exits 0; existing policies are NOT duplicated; no data loss. The earlier 'PROVISION-RERUN-001 duplicate CREATE POLICY abort' prediction was FALSE for this turn.
turns.hybrid_path: day2-driver add-column recipes.prep_minutes:numeric -> pnpm codegen -> scaffold-frontend --refresh-entity recipes -> day2-verify
turns.new_capability_assert: create a recipe with prep_minutes=30, reload the page, and the value both PERSISTS (round-trips from the DB) AND RENDERS in the recipe list/detail.

dual_assertion.new_capability: a recipe created with prep_minutes=30 persists and renders after reload
dual_assertion.regression: the email-password flow still round-trips (signup -> create recipe -> reload -> assert persisted + authed) on /recipes with the recipe-* testids

turns.title: new entity + relation
turns.change_request: Let a recipe have a list of ingredients. Add an ingredients entity where each ingredient belongs to exactly one recipe (the recipe is required), and is private to its owner like recipes.
turns.brief_patch: Add a new owner-scoped table `ingredients` with fields { name: text required } and { quantity: text }, and add a RelationBelongsTo from ingredients -> recipes via a REQUIRED FK column recipe_id (delete_action CASCADE). Add a CRUD ui.route { path: /ingredients, label: Ingredients, kind: crud, entity: ingredient }.
turns.skill_only_path: edit brief -> scaffold-provision -> (cd packages/provision) pnpm run provision. (Steps only — NOT a verdict prediction. The runner decides the verdict from the live did-it-land probe + dual-verify.)
turns.verified_outcome: clean. VERIFIED (Stage-B, warm hub): a NEW owner-scoped table + a REQUIRED-FK relation are ADDITIVE — they land skill-only on the already-provisioned DB. Confirmed in the live tenant: the `ingredients` table exists (information_schema.tables) and its `recipe_id` FK column exists (information_schema.columns). Re-provisioning the existing recipes table is idempotent (no duplicate-policy abort).
turns.hybrid_path: day2-driver add-table ingredients (owner) -> day2-driver add-relation ingredients.recipe_id->recipes required CASCADE -> pnpm codegen -> scaffold-frontend --add-entity ingredient -> day2-verify
turns.new_capability_assert: create a recipe, then create an ingredient that picks that recipe via the required recipe_id FK picker (camelCase recipeId spread into the create), reload, and the ingredient PERSISTS bound to its parent recipe; a create with NO recipe selected is rejected (the FK is NOT NULL).

dual_assertion.new_capability: an ingredient created against a parent recipe (required recipe_id FK) persists and renders bound to that recipe; an ingredient create with no parent is rejected
dual_assertion.regression: the email-password flow still round-trips on the original recipes entity (recipe-* testids), and recipes still CRUD-persist

turns.title: RLS policy flip
turns.change_request: Make recipes shareable: a published recipe should be readable by any signed-in user, but only its author may create, edit, or unpublish it. Ingredients stay private to their owner.
turns.brief_patch: Change data_model.tables[name=recipes].policy from `owner` to `public-read+owner-write`. This swaps the single AuthzDirectOwner policy for the two-policy stack (AuthzDirectOwner all-CRUD + AuthzPublishable select-only) and adds the DataPublishable node (is_published / published_at). Leave ingredients on policy: owner. No preset change — public-read+owner-write works on auth:email (no b2b).
turns.skill_only_path: edit brief -> scaffold-provision -> (cd packages/provision) pnpm run provision. (Steps only — NOT a verdict prediction. The runner decides the verdict from the live did-it-land probe + dual-verify.)
turns.verified_outcome: impossible (skill-only) on the VERIFIED cause — NOT a duplicate-policy abort. VERIFIED (Stage-B): re-provision is idempotent for the policy stack itself (the AuthzPublishable SELECT policy + the additive DataPublishable columns is_published/published_at CAN land additively — confirmed in the live tenant: pg_policies shows auth_sel_publishable on recipes, and both columns are present). The REAL day-2 wall is the NOT-NULL backfill: turning an EXISTING populated table public-readable wants is_published NOT NULL, and adding a NOT-NULL column to a table that already has rows fails ('column "is_published" contains null values' / NOT-NULL backfill) unless the column is added NULLABLE or WITH A DEFAULT and backfilled. NOTE: this may be FIXED by C1 (if C1 emits is_published as nullable / DEFAULT false and backfills) — in which case the runner's did-it-land probe will report landed=yes and the verdict becomes clean. The runner measures whichever is true; it does not assume. (The earlier 'duplicate CREATE POLICY' prediction was FALSE and is removed.)
turns.hybrid_path: day2-driver set-policy recipes public-read+owner-write (adds the DataPublishable columns is_published NULLABLE/DEFAULT false + published_at and backfills, applies AuthzDirectOwner all-CRUD + AuthzPublishable select-only) -> pnpm codegen -> scaffold-frontend --refresh-entity recipes -> day2-verify
turns.new_capability_assert: user A publishes a recipe (is_published=true); a SECOND user B can READ user A's published recipe; user A (owner) can still write/unpublish it; user B (non-owner) still CANNOT write it; ingredients remain owner-private (B cannot read A's ingredients).

dual_assertion.new_capability: a published recipe is readable by a second user while writes stay owner-locked (non-owner write denied), and ingredients stay owner-private
dual_assertion.regression: the email-password flow still round-trips for the OWNER on /recipes (owner can still create/read/edit their own recipes; recipe-* testids)

turns.title: add a flow
turns.change_request: Add a self-serve password reset: a user who forgot their password can request a reset email and set a new password from the emailed link.
turns.brief_patch: Append `password-reset` to the top-level flows list (alongside email-password). password-reset rides the same auth:email base — it adds ZERO new modules (its backend.modules are identical to email-password's auth:email 13-module set) and contributes the forgotPassword/resetPassword ops + the forgot-password / reset-password Blocks. Do NOT add it to acceptance.required_flows (no live-QA driver for it yet — email-password stays the only acceptance flow).
turns.skill_only_path: edit brief -> scaffold-provision -> (cd packages/provision) pnpm run provision. (Steps only — NOT a verdict prediction. The runner decides the verdict from the live did-it-land probe + dual-verify.)
turns.verified_outcome: partial. VERIFIED (Stage-B): password-reset adds ZERO new modules (its backend.modules are byte-identical to email-password's auth:email 13-module set), so the re-provision leg is a NO-OP / not-applicable — there is no schema change to land (the did-it-land probe reports landed=n/a: no DB-checkable op). The real change is purely the FRONTEND surface (the forgot-password / reset-password Blocks install + the forgotPassword/resetPassword ops). Those Blocks install fine; what the skill-only path does NOT do hands-free is wire+codegen them onto the already-built app so the new capability round-trips end-to-end (Mailpit link -> resetPassword -> sign in with new password). So: backing/regression intact, new capability not driven skill-only => PARTIAL. (The earlier 'duplicate CREATE POLICY abort before wiring Blocks' prediction was FALSE — there are no new policies to replay; the re-provision simply has nothing to do.)
turns.hybrid_path: day2-driver add-flow password-reset (union its modules into create-db — a no-op here since password-reset adds none; wire forgot-password/reset-password Blocks + routes) -> pnpm codegen -> scaffold-frontend --add-flow password-reset -> day2-verify
turns.new_capability_assert: the password-reset happy path round-trips: request a reset for the signed-up email (forgotPassword) -> open the reset link from Mailpit (:8025) -> set a new password (resetPassword) -> sign in with the NEW password succeeds and the OLD password fails.

dual_assertion.new_capability: password-reset happy path round-trips end-to-end (forgotPassword -> Mailpit link -> resetPassword -> sign in with new password works, old fails)
dual_assertion.regression: the email-password flow still round-trips (signup + sign-in + create recipe + reload) unchanged
